name: Advance America
description_short: Advance America offers payday, installment, and title loans up to $25,000 in-store and online, with same-day funding and bilingual service.
description_long:
Advance America is a nationally recognized consumer lending chain with a physical presence across Florida and beyond. The Jacksonville North Edgewood location serves the Commonwealth Village area, positioned west of downtown near the I-95/I-295 corridor. The company markets itself as a fully accredited lender serving millions of Americans who need fast access to cash, and has accumulated over 200,000 Google reviews with a 4.9 out of 5 aggregate rating — a volume that reflects significant transaction scale rather than a boutique operation.

The store offers three core loan products. Payday Loans range from $100 to $500 and are structured as two-to-four-week short-term loans designed to bridge gaps between paychecks. Installment Loans range from $100 to $1,000 and allow repayment in multiple payments over time, offering more flexibility than a single-balloon payday product. Title Loans, offered in partnership with LoanCenter (not directly underwritten by Advance America), range from $2,000 to $25,000 and require the borrower's vehicle as collateral — the vehicle must be paid off, registered in the borrower's name, and the title must be in their possession. The branch also provides Western Union money transfer services.

What distinguishes this location is its bilingual staffing — the store explicitly advertises Spanish-language service, with signage in both English and Spanish. Applications can be completed online or in-store, and phone pre-qualification is available. Requirements are straightforward: a government-issued ID, proof of income, a checking account, and a Social Security Number or ITIN. Friday and Saturday hours open earlier (9:00 am) than the weekday standard, though Sunday hours are unavailable.

Advance America occupies the high-cost end of the consumer lending spectrum. Payday and installment loans at this type of lender typically carry triple-digit APRs, and the title loan product puts a vehicle at risk of repossession if payments are missed. The partnership model for title loans means the terms and servicing ultimately flow through LoanCenter, adding a layer of separation from the branded storefront. This is a last-resort or bridge-financing option — appropriate for consumers with no access to credit union alternatives or bank personal loans, but carries meaningful financial risk if not repaid quickly.
pros: 4.9/5 aggregate rating across 125,409+ verified reviews — unusually high volume of customer feedback
Bilingual (English/Spanish) staff on-site for Spanish-speaking borrowers
Three distinct loan products covering $100 to $25,000 in a single location
Same-day funding available on personal loans up to $1,000
Both online and in-store application paths available
Phone pre-qualification offered before visiting the branch
Western Union money transfers available in-store alongside lending
cons: Payday loans are two-to-four-week balloon products — structure encourages rollovers if not repaid on schedule
Title loans are underwritten by a third party (LoanCenter), not directly by Advance America — borrowers are dealing with two entities
Title loans require a fully paid-off vehicle with clear title, excluding borrowers still making car payments
No Sunday hours limits access for borrowers with weekday work constraints
High-cost product category — APRs on payday and installment loans at this type of lender are typically far above 100%
best_for: Employed borrowers facing a one-time cash shortfall before their next paycheck with a clear repayment plan
Vehicle owners with a paid-off car who need $2,000–$25,000 and cannot qualify for a bank personal loan
Spanish-speaking borrowers who need in-person bilingual assistance navigating a loan product
Consumers who have already ruled out credit union PALs or CDFI alternatives and need same-day cash
diagnosis: Advance America is best suited for employed borrowers with no access to lower-cost credit who need $100–$1,000 same-day and can repay within one to two pay cycles, or vehicle owners needing a larger lump sum via title loan. The central caveat is cost: payday and short-term installment loans at this type of lender carry very high APRs, and the title loan product carries repossession risk — borrowers who cannot repay quickly will find the cost compounds rapidly.
services: Title Loans ($2,000–$25,000, in partnership with LoanCenter)
Same-day personal loan funding up to $1,000
